definitions.example: Fred won't be able to come to the party; he has a meeting that day.

definitions.definition: To experience, go through, undergo.
definitions.example: He had surgery on his hip yesterday.

definitions.definition: To be afflicted with, suffer from.
definitions.example: He had a cold last week.

definitions.definition: (auxiliary verb, taking a past participle) Used in forming the perfect aspect.
definitions.example: I had already eaten.

definitions.definition: Used as an interrogative verb before a pronoun to form a tag question, echoing a previous use of 'have' as an auxiliary verb or, in certain cases, main verb. (For further discussion, see the appendix English tag questions.)
definitions.example: They haven't eaten dinner yet, have they?

definitions.definition: (auxiliary verb, taking a to-infinitive) See have to.
definitions.example: I have to go.

definitions.definition: To give birth to.
definitions.example: My mother had me when she was 25.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with.
definitions.example: He's always bragging about how many women he's had.

definitions.definition: To accept as a romantic partner.
definitions.example: Despite my protestations of love, she would not have me.

definitions.definition: (transitive with bare infinitive) To cause to, by a command, request or invitation.
definitions.example: They had me feed their dog while they were out of town.

definitions.definition: (transitive with adjective or adjective-phrase complement) To cause to be.
definitions.example: He had him arrested for trespassing.

definitions.definition: (transitive with bare infinitive) To be affected by an occurrence. (Used in supplying a topic that is not a verb argument.)
definitions.example: I've had three people today tell me my hair looks nice.

definitions.definition: (transitive with adjective or adjective-phrase complement) To depict as being.
definitions.example: Their stories differed; he said he'd been at work when the incident occurred, but her statement had him at home that entire evening.

definitions.definition: To defeat in a fight; take.
definitions.example: I could have him!

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside Ireland) To be able to speak (a language).
definitions.example: I have no German.

definitions.definition: To feel or be (especially painfully) aware of.
definitions.example: Dan certainly has arms today, probably from scraping paint off four columns the day before.

definitions.definition: To trick, to deceive.
definitions.example: You had me alright! I never would have thought that was just a joke.

definitions.definition: (often with present participle) To allow; to tolerate.
definitions.example: I asked my dad if I could go to the concert this Thursday, but he wouldn't have it since it's a school night.

definitions.definition: (often used in the negative) To believe, buy, be taken in by.
definitions.example: I made up an excuse as to why I was out so late, but my wife wasn't having any of it.

definitions.definition: To host someone; to take in as a guest.
definitions.example: Thank you for having me!

definitions.definition: To get a reading, measurement, or result from an instrument or calculation.
definitions.example: I have two contacts on my scope.

definitions.definition: (of a jury) To consider a court proceeding that has been completed; to begin deliberations on a case.
definitions.example: We'll schedule closing arguments for Thursday, and the jury will have the case by that afternoon.

definitions.definition: To make an observation of (a bird species).
A horizontal structural member of post and beam architecture, typically attached to bridge two or more vertical members such as corner posts.
To gird.
To bind horizontally, as with a belt or girdle.
To measure the girth of.
To bind with a flexible rope or cord.
definitions.example: The fasces were girt about with twine in bundles large.

definitions.definition: To encircle with, or as if with a belt.
definitions.example: The lady girt herself with silver chain, from which she hung a golden shear.

definitions.definition: To prepare oneself for an action.
To capsize because of forces in the cable attaching it to another vessel.
Bound by a cable; used of a vessel so moored by two anchors that she swings against one of the cables by force of the current or tide.
Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: A graven image or representation of anything that is revered, or believed to convey spiritual power.
A cultural icon, or especially popular person.
(originally Japan) Popular entertainer; usually young, captivating, attractive; and often female, with an image of being close to fans.
An eidolon or phantom; something misleading or elusive.
To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.
definitions.example: You should sleep 8 hours a day.

definitions.definition: (of a spinning top or yo-yo) To spin on its axis with no other perceptible motion.
To cause (a spinning top or yo-yo) to spin on its axis with no other perceptible motion.
To accommodate in beds.
definitions.example: This caravan can sleep four people comfortably.

definitions.definition: To be slumbering in (a state).
definitions.example: to sleep a dreamless sleep

definitions.definition: To be careless, inattentive, or unconcerned; not to be vigilant; to live thoughtlessly.
To be dead; to lie in the grave.
To be, or appear to be, in repose; to be quiet; to be unemployed, unused, or unagitated; to rest; to lie dormant.
definitions.example: a question sleeps for the present; the law sleeps

definitions.definition: To wait for a period of time without performing any action.
definitions.example: After a failed connection attempt, the program sleeps for 5 seconds before trying again.

definitions.definition: To place into a state of hibernation.
The state of reduced consciousness during which a human or animal rests in a daily rhythm.
definitions.example: I really need some sleep.

definitions.definition: An act or instance of sleeping.
definitions.example: I’m just going to have a quick sleep.

definitions.definition: (metonymically) A night.
definitions.example: There are only three sleeps till Christmas!

definitions.definition: Rheum, crusty or gummy discharge found in the corner of the eyes after waking, whether real or a figurative objectification of sleep (in the sense of reduced consciousness).
definitions.example: Wipe the sleep from your eyes.

definitions.definition: A state of plants, usually at night, when their leaflets approach each other and the flowers close and droop, or are covered by the folded leaves.
The hibernation of animals.
The currency of Uzbekistan.
The currency of Kyrgyzstan.
A certain number, at least two.
definitions.example: Some enjoy spicy food, others prefer it milder.

definitions.definition: An indefinite quantity.
definitions.example: Can I have some of them?

definitions.definition: An indefinite amount, a part.
definitions.example: Everyone is wrong some of the time.

definitions.definition: (radio telecommunications) Received (used in radio communications to acknowledge that a message has been received and understood)
Of a man, to have sexual intercourse with (someone), especially in a rough manner.
To have sexual intercourse.
The sound of a light slap or tap with a soft flat object, especially of a footstep
A light tap or slap, especially with the hands
A flattish lump of soft matter, especially butter or dung.
To (gently) tap the flat of one's hand on a person or thing.
definitions.example: To show affection, he decided he would pat the boy on the head.

definitions.definition: To hit lightly and repeatedly with the flat of the hand to make smooth or flat
definitions.example: I patted the cookie dough into shape.

definitions.definition: To stroke or fondle (an animal).
definitions.example: Do you want to pat the cat?

definitions.definition: To gently rain.
Timely, suitable, apt, opportune, ready for the occasion; especially of things spoken.
definitions.example: a pat expression

definitions.definition: Trite, being superficially complete, lacking originality.
Opportunely, in a timely or suitable way.
Perfectly.
definitions.example: He has the routine down pat.

definitions.definition: Patent.
Pattern.
A genre of popular music mainly associated with Mauritius.
(in Europe) A transfer of funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
An unemployment benefit cheque.
To transfer funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
